---
pagetitle: "Research supervision | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Current and completed doctoral and MPhil supervision by Dr Mesfin Genie, and guidance for prospective research students."
title-block-style: none
page-layout: full
image: /assets/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-header"><div class="page-shell">
<p class="eyebrow">Teaching and supervision</p>
<h1>Research supervision</h1>
<p>As a <strong>principal supervisor</strong> I take lead responsibility for a candidate's programme; as a <strong>co-supervisor</strong> I share supervision with a lead supervisor, usually contributing health-economics and choice-modelling expertise.</p>
</div></header>

<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow">

<h2>Current supervision</h2>
<div class="event-list">
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">Understanding the Drivers of Telehealth Usage Among Patients in Australia and How It Reduces Costs Relative to In-Person Healthcare Services</div><div class="publication-meta">Principal Supervisor · Doctor of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Current</div></article>
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">Economic Analysis of the Impact of Climate Change on the Agricultural Sector in the Solomon Islands</div><div class="publication-meta">Co-Supervisor · Doctor of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Expected 31 December 2028</div></article>
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">Economic Modelling of the Impact of Novel Approaches for Crop Drought Resilience Using Long-Term Trials in Australia</div><div class="publication-meta">Co-Supervisor · Doctor of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Current</div></article>
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">Gendered Ageism in the Workplace: Menopause and Female Labour Market Participation</div><div class="publication-meta">Co-Supervisor · Doctor of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Current</div></article>
</div>

<h2>Completed supervision</h2>
<div class="event-list">
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">Health Systems Reform Dynamics: Implications for Equity and Efficiency</div><div class="publication-meta">Co-Supervisor · Doctor of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Completed 13 March 2025</div></article>
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">Socio-Economic and Behavioural Impacts of COVID-19 Vaccine Mandates: A Scoping Review</div><div class="publication-meta">Principal Supervisor · Master of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Recorded 10 November 2024</div></article>
<article class="publication-record"><div class="publication-title">The Socio-Economic and Behavioural Impacts of Influenza Vaccine Mandates on Healthcare Workers: A Scoping Review</div><div class="publication-meta">Principal Supervisor · Master of Philosophy · The University of Newcastle, Australia · Recorded 10 November 2024</div></article>
</div>
<p class="grant-note">The two MPhil scoping reviews show a recorded date of 10 November 2024; this is listed as a record date pending confirmation that it is the completion date.</p>

<h2>Supervision philosophy</h2>
<p>I aim to help students ask a clear question, choose a method that can answer it, and communicate the result to the people who can use it. My areas of interest for supervision are health economics, discrete choice experiments, eye-tracking and information-processing methods, clinical outcomes assessment, behavioural economics, and health policy.</p>

<h2>Prospective students</h2>
<p>If you would like to discuss supervision, please send a CV setting out your academic qualifications, GPA or grading information, research experience, and any publications, along with a short note on the questions you would like to work on. Enquiries are welcome but supervision is selective and depends on fit with current projects.</p>
<p><a class="button-secondary" href="/contact/">Contact for supervision enquiries</a></p>

</div></section>
```
